The '''75th British Academy Film Awards''', also known as the [[British Academy Film Awards|BAFTAs]], were held on 13 March 2022 at the [[Royal Albert Hall]] in [[London]], honouring the best national and foreign films of [[2021 in film|2021]].<ref name="Heritage">{{cite web |last=Heritage |first=Stuart |date=13 March 2022 |title=Baftas 2022: Britain's big film awards night – as it happened |work=[[The Guardian]] |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/live/2022/mar/13/baftas-2022-britains-big-film-awards-night-live |access-date=13 March 2022 |issn=0261-3077 |url-access=limited |archive-date=13 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220313184728/https://www.theguardian.com/film/live/2022/mar/13/baftas-2022-britains-big-film-awards-night-live |url-status=live}}</ref> Presented by the [[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]], accolades were handed out for the best feature-length film and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at British cinemas in 2021.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/bafta-2022-film-awards-date-1234971132/ |title=BAFTA Sets Date for 2022 Film Awards |first=Alex |last=Ritman |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |date=21 June 2021 |access-date=21 June 2021 |archive-date=9 December 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211209121723/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/bafta-2022-film-awards-date-1234971132/ |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite press release |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/2022-date-ee-british-academy-film-awards |title=BAFTA sets date for 2022 EE British Academy Film Awards |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]] |date=21 June 2021 |access-date=3 February 2022 |archive-date=22 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220122064520/https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/2022-date-ee-british-academy-film-awards |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite press release |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/bafta-rules-and-timeline-for-ee-british-academy-film-awards-2022 |title=BAFTA publishes rules and timeline for the EE British Academy Film Awards in 2022 |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]] |date=10 September 2021 |access-date=3 February 2022 |archive-date=18 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220218061045/https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/bafta-rules-and-timeline-for-ee-british-academy-film-awards-2022 |url-status=live}}</ref>

The nominations were announced on 3 February 2022.<ref name="Nominations">{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2022/film/news/bafta-2022-awards-nominations-full-list-1235170992/ |title=BAFTA Awards Nominations Unveiled: 'Dune', 'Power of the Dog' Lead Field, Will Smith Earns First BAFTA Nod |first=Manori |last=Ravindran |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=3 February 2022 |access-date=3 February 2022 |archive-date=3 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220203124204/https://variety.com/2022/film/news/bafta-2022-awards-nominations-full-list-1235170992/ |url-status=live}}</ref> The [[BAFTA Rising Star Award|Rising Star Award]] nominees, which is the only category voted for by the British public, were announced on 1 February 2022; the nomination and eventual win of [[Lashana Lynch]] confused commentators, who did not consider her "rising".<ref name="Heritage"/><ref>{{cite press release |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/2022-ee-rising-star-award-nominees-announced |title=Nominees announced for the 2022 EE Rising Star Award ahead of the EE BAFTA Film Awards |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]] |date=1 February 2022 |access-date=3 February 2022 |archive-date=3 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220203075733/https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/2022-ee-rising-star-award-nominees-announced |url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="Dex">{{cite web |last=Dex |first=Robert |date=13 March 2022 |title=Bafta awards 2022: Lashana Lynch named Rising Star |url=https://www.standard.co.uk/news/uk/lashana-lynch-royal-albert-hall-2022-baftas-dune-b987807.html |access-date=13 March 2022 |work=[[Evening Standard]] |archive-date=13 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220313184456/https://www.standard.co.uk/news/uk/lashana-lynch-royal-albert-hall-2022-baftas-dune-b987807.html |url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="NME">{{cite web |last=Levine |first=Nick |date=13 March 2022 |title=The biggest talking points from the BAFTAs 2022 |url=https://www.nme.com/features/film-features/baftas-2022-talking-points-shock-snub-lady-gaga-3181403 |access-date=14 March 2022 |work=[[NME]] |archive-date=14 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220314004043/https://www.nme.com/features/film-features/baftas-2022-talking-points-shock-snub-lady-gaga-3181403 |url-status=live}}</ref>

The [[Epic film|epic]] [[science fiction film]] ''[[Dune (2021 film)|Dune]]'' received the most nominations with eleven; ''[[The Power of the Dog (film)|The Power of the Dog]]'' and ''[[Belfast (film)|Belfast]]'' followed with eight and six, respectively.<ref name="Nominations"/> The former ultimately won two—[[BAFTA Award for Best Film|Best Film]] and [[BAFTA Award for Best Direction|Best Director]] ([[Jane Campion]]); ''Dune'' received the most wins, with five, but did not take home any major category awards.<ref name="Heritage"/><ref name="NME"/><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2022/mar/13/baftas-2022-the-power-of-the-dog-wins-best-picture-and-director |title=Baftas 2022: The Power of the Dog wins best picture and director |last=Shoard |first=Catherine |work=[[The Guardian]] |date=13 March 2022 |access-date=15 March 2022 |url-access=limited}}</ref>

The ceremony was hosted by actress and comedian [[Rebel Wilson]].<ref>{{cite press release |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/rebel-wilson-ceremony-host-2022-ee-british-academy-film-awards |title=Rebel Wilson Announced as Ceremony Host for EE British Academy Film Awards in 2022 |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]] |date=14 January 2022 |access-date=15 March 2022 |archive-date=27 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220127022335/https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/rebel-wilson-ceremony-host-2022-ee-british-academy-film-awards |url-status=live}}</ref> Many winners were not present to collect their awards, though the ceremony was exclusively in-person. This allowed the speeches of winners who were present to go on at length. While many awards were won by frontrunners in the [[Academy Awards|Oscars]] race, a surprise win came in the [[BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Leading Role|Best Actress]] category – already populated by no Oscar nominees – for [[Wales|Welsh]] actress [[Joanna Scanlan]] for her performance in ''[[After Love (2020 film)|After Love]]''.<ref name="Heritage"/><ref name="NME"/><ref name="Colderick">{{cite web |last=Colderick |first=Stephanie |date=13 March 2022 |title=Joanna Scanlan thanks BAFTA in Welsh as she accepts award for leading actress |url=https://www.walesonline.co.uk/lifestyle/tv/joanna-scanlan-thanks-bafta-welsh-23377065 |access-date=14 March 2022 |publisher=[[Media Wales|WalesOnline]] |archive-date=13 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220313231000/https://www.walesonline.co.uk/lifestyle/tv/joanna-scanlan-thanks-bafta-welsh-23377065 |url-status=live}}</ref>

The nominees were announced on 3 February 2022.<ref>{{cite press release |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/film-awards-nominations-announced-2022 |title=Nominations Announced: EE British Academy Film Awards in 2022 |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]] |date=3 February 2022 |access-date=3 February 2022 |archive-date=3 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220203130733/https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/film-awards-nominations-announced-2022 |url-status=live}}</ref> The winners were announced on 13 March 2022.<ref>{{cite press release |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/bafta-film-awards-winners-announced |title=EE British Academy Film Awards: Winners Announced |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]] |date=13 March 2022 |access-date=15 March 2022 |archive-date=14 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220314123724/https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/bafta-film-awards-winners-announced |url-status=live}}</ref>

This year, BAFTA did not hand out two of its honorary awards—[[BAFTA Fellowship]] and [[BAFTA Outstanding British Contribution to Cinema Award]]—as it plans to implement new [[vetting]] processes following the [[74th British Academy Film Awards|previous year's]] controversy surrounding [[Noel Clarke]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2022/01/bafta-pauses-honorary-film-awards-2022-noel-clarke-controversy-1234918399/ |title=BAFTA Won't Give Out Honorary Film Awards in 2022 After Noel Clarke Controversy |first=Tom |last=Grater |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=24 January 2022 |access-date=3 February 2022 |archive-date=3 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220203130941/https://deadline.com/2022/01/bafta-pauses-honorary-film-awards-2022-noel-clarke-controversy-1234918399/ |url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="BBC News">{{cite web |date=13 March 2022 |title=Bafta Film Awards: Benedict Cumberbatch's The Power of the Dog wins top prize |publisher=[[BBC News]] |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/entertainment-arts-60675263 |access-date=14 March 2022 |archive-date=14 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220314000005/https://www.bbc.com/news/entertainment-arts-60675263 |url-status=live}}</ref>

==Ceremony information==
[[File:Rebel Wilson 2020 BAFTA Film Awards.png|thumb|[[Rebel Wilson]] presenting at the [[73rd British Academy Film Awards]] in 2020]]

The ceremony was broadcast on [[BBC One]], [[BBC One#BBC One HD|BBC One HD]] and [[BBC iPlayer]] at 7:00 p.m on 13 March 2022, the day of the ceremony, and was available to stream exclusively on [[BritBox]] in North America and South Africa simultaneously with the UK broadcast.<ref>{{cite press release |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/britbox-to-stream-baftas-for-us-south-african-members |title=BritBox to be the exclusive home in North America & South Africa for the 75th EE British Academy Film Awards |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]] |date=1 March 2022 |access-date=11 March 2022 |archive-date=11 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220311060955/https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/britbox-to-stream-baftas-for-us-south-african-members |url-status=live}}</ref> The broadcast was not live with the awards presentation, and some parts were edited out, including host [[Rebel Wilson]] ranting about a film production gone awry and cursing the production company.<ref name="Heritage"/> Wilson was selected as host after having presented an award at the previous in-person [[73rd British Academy Film Awards|BAFA ceremony in February 2020]]; in his live coverage of the 2022 ceremony for ''[[The Guardian]]'', British film journalist Stuart Heritage wrote that Wilson had been the only funny part of the 2020 ceremony.<ref name="Heritage"/>

While Heritage enjoyed many parts of Wilson's opening monologue at the 2022 ceremony, including jokes about the demise of the [[British Academy Film Awards|BAF(T)As]] and her own weight loss, he was unimpressed with her wordplay regarding [[Tom Hiddleston]]'s "low key" (''[[Loki (TV series)|Loki]]'') appearance at the ceremony; Heritage went on to note throughout the broadcast that Wilson's various skits (including drawing nipples on a flip chart, inviting [[Andy Serkis]] to defile a cake shaped like [[Benedict Cumberbatch]], and throwing her bras into the audience) were bad.<ref name="Heritage"/> ''[[NME]]'' wrote that, while the hosting was troubled, Wilson improved towards the end of the ceremony, particularly her [[one-line joke|one-liners]].<ref name="NME"/> [[Digital Spy]], by collecting popular [[Twitter|tweets]] about the ceremony, reported that many viewers were off-put by Wilson's unfunny hosting, though some enjoyed the fact she did not hold back when it came to topical humour.<ref>{{cite web |last=Hooton |first=Kayleigh |date=13 March 2022 |title=BAFTA host Rebel Wilson gets a mixed reaction from viewers |url=https://www.digitalspy.com/movies/a39420965/bafta-host-rebel-wilson-mixed-reaction-from-viewers/ |access-date=14 March 2022 |publisher=[[Digital Spy]] |archive-date=14 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220314005057/https://www.digitalspy.com/movies/a39420965/bafta-host-rebel-wilson-mixed-reaction-from-viewers/ |url-status=live}}</ref> The [[BBC]] (which broadcast the ceremony) agreed that Wilson's monologue and one-liners were her strongest points, writing that some of her other jokes fell flat, but they thought "on the whole [she] did a fine job".<ref>{{cite web |last=McIntosh |first=Steven |date=14 March 2022 |title=Baftas 2022: Seven highlights - from the jokes to the dresses |publisher=[[BBC News]] |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/entertainment-arts-60730808 |access-date=14 March 2022 |archive-date=14 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220314020923/https://www.bbc.com/news/entertainment-arts-60730808 |url-status=live}}</ref> ''[[The Daily Telegraph|The Telegraph]]'' was instead highly negative of Wilson's hosting and the mismatched tone of the ceremony she created, but praised her ability to "plough on" anyway; it also noted that in coming on after a strong opening song, Wilson was already at a disadvantage.<ref name="Power">{{cite web |last=Power |first=Ed |date=13 March 2022 |title=Baftas 2022 review: host Rebel Wilson's wrecking-ball comedy included one stone-cold clunker that drew boos |work=[[The Daily Telegraph|The Telegraph]] |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/films/0/baftas-2022-review-host-rebel-wilsons-wrecking-ball-comedy-made/ |access-date=14 March 2022 |issn=0307-1235 |url-access=subscription |archive-date=13 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220313225417/https://www.telegraph.co.uk/films/0/baftas-2022-review-host-rebel-wilsons-wrecking-ball-comedy-made/ |url-status=live}}</ref>

The ''[[de facto]]'' theme of the ceremony was "all things [[Portrayal of James Bond in film|Bond]]".<ref>{{cite web |date=13 March 2022 |title=Lashana Lynch's Prada BAFTAs Look is a Tribute to Black Hollywood Legends |url=https://www.vogue.co.uk/news/article/lashana-lynch-baftas-2022 |access-date=14 March 2022 |work=[[British Vogue]] |archive-date=13 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220313180120/https://www.vogue.co.uk/news/article/lashana-lynch-baftas-2022 |url-status=live}}</ref> Opening the ceremony, to mark the 60th anniversary of the [[Production of the James Bond films|''James Bond'' franchise]], Dame [[Shirley Bassey]], who is connected with the franchise after contributing to the soundtrack of [[List of James Bond films|multiple Bond films]] throughout her career, performed a rendition of the title song from ''[[Diamonds Are Forever (film)|Diamonds Are Forever]]'' (1971), the seventh installment of the franchise, with ''[[NME]]'' praising that her voice was as good as ever, writing: "At 85, her voice remains fully capable of filling the [[Royal Albert Hall]] to the rafters."<ref name="NME"/><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/bafta-awards-2022-shirley-bassey-bond-1235110158/ |title=Shirley Bassey Opens BAFTA Awards with 'Diamonds Are Forever' |first=Alex |last=Ritman |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |date=13 March 2022 |access-date=15 March 2022 |archive-date=14 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220314054445/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/bafta-awards-2022-shirley-bassey-bond-1235110158/ |url-status=live}}</ref> Before the ceremony, it was announced that Bassey would perform an iconic [[James Bond music|Bond theme]], only to be revealed on the night.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2022/03/bafta-film-awards-shirley-bassey-perform-james-bond-theme-1234972173/ |title=Dame Shirley Bassey to Open BAFTA Film Awards Ceremony: It Will Be a James Bond Theme, Choice Staying Secret |first=Caroline |last=Frost |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=6 March 2022 |access-date=6 March 2022 |archive-date=6 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220306195026/https://deadline.com/2022/03/bafta-film-awards-shirley-bassey-perform-james-bond-theme-1234972173/ |url-status=live}}</ref> Also performing live during the ceremony was English actress and singer [[Emilia Jones]], nominated for [[BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Leading Role|Best Actress in a Leading Role]] for her performance in ''[[CODA (2021 film)|CODA]]''.<ref>{{cite AV media |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NRAL-axamaU |title=Emilia Jones - Both Sides Now (Full Performance - Live at the 75th British Academy Film Awards) |publisher=Emilia Jones Videos |via=[[YouTube]] |date=31 March 2022 |access-date=1 March 2023}}</ref> She performed a rendition of [[Joni Mitchell]]'s "[[Both Sides, Now]]", which her character sings in the film; she was joined by two [[translation|translators]] signing the song in [[American Sign Language]] (ASL) and [[British Sign Language]] (BSL).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.walesonline.co.uk/lifestyle/tv/emilia-jones-viewers-sobbing-over-23376815 |title=Emilia Jones has viewers 'sobbing' over emotional BAFTA performance |last=Colderick |first=Stephanie |publisher=[[Media Wales|WalesOnline]] |date=13 March 2022 |access-date=15 March 2022 |archive-date=15 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220315055615/https://www.walesonline.co.uk/lifestyle/tv/emilia-jones-viewers-sobbing-over-23376815 |url-status=live}}</ref>

Jones' performance was introduced by Wilson, who, while welcoming the interpreters, joked that the sign for "[[Vladimir Putin]]" was instead universal, holding up a [[The finger|middle finger]] to the camera.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/arts-entertainment/films/news/rebel-wilson-putin-ukraine-baftas-2022-b2035089.html |title=Baftas 2022: Rebel Wilson draws cheers with Putin joke |last=Harrison |first=Ellie |work=[[The Independent]] |date=13 March 2022 |access-date=15 March 2022 |archive-date=14 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220314130656/https://www.independent.co.uk/arts-entertainment/films/news/rebel-wilson-putin-ukraine-baftas-2022-b2035089.html |url-status=live}}</ref> The same gesture had been made in a different context a week earlier at the [[37th Independent Spirit Awards]] by its hosts, [[Megan Mullally]] and [[Nick Offerman]], and presenter [[Kristen Stewart]].<ref>{{cite web |last=Starkey |first=Adam |date=7 March 2022 |title=Kristen Stewart and Nick Offerman give middle finger to Putin at Independent Spirit Awards |url=https://www.nme.com/news/film/kristen-stewart-nick-offerman-megan-mullally-give-middle-finger-to-putin-independent-spirit-awards-3177156 |access-date=13 March 2022 |work=[[NME]] |archive-date=7 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220307140526/https://www.nme.com/news/film/kristen-stewart-nick-offerman-megan-mullally-give-middle-finger-to-putin-independent-spirit-awards-3177156 |url-status=live}}</ref> Deaf journalist Liam O'Dell wrote that Wilson's version was offensive to sign language users as it reduced their language and accessibility to a punchline.<ref>{{cite web |last=O'Dell |first=Liam |date=14 March 2022 |title=Opinion: Rebel Wilson's Putin gag at the Baftas wasn't funny – it was offensive |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/voices/rebel-wilson-baftas-putin-joke-offensive-b2035231.html |access-date=14 March 2022 |work=[[The Independent]] |archive-date=14 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220314134830/https://www.independent.co.uk/voices/rebel-wilson-baftas-putin-joke-offensive-b2035231.html |url-status=live}}</ref> Other political humour included Wilson saying she cut a planned musical number about [[Prince Andrew, Duke of York|Prince Andrew]] called "[[Prince Andrew & the Epstein Scandal|Pizza Express]]" and a lengthy but earnest joke Serkis made about the policies of [[Home Secretary]] [[Priti Patel]] while introducing the award for [[BAFTA Award for Best Direction|Best Director]].<ref name="Heritage"/><ref name="Dex"/><ref name="Power"/> Serious statements regarding the [[Russian invasion of Ukraine]] were also made by Wilson, following the ''In Memoriam'' montage, and by BAFTA Chair Krishnendu Majumdar in his opening speech, condemning the [[Russian invasion of Ukraine|Russian invasion]] and reading a message from the [[Ukrainian Film Academy]].<ref name="Dex"/><ref name="BBC News"/> For the second year in a row, [[William, Prince of Wales|Prince William, Duke of Cambridge]], BAFTA President, did not attend the ceremony, instead delivering a speech via a pre-recorded video that aired during the ceremony.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2022/film/news/prince-william-bafta-awards-2022-1235201205/ |title=BAFTA President Prince William Will Not Attend Sunday's BAFTA Awards |first=K.J. |last=Yossman |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=10 March 2022 |access-date=11 March 2022 |archive-date=10 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220310213404/https://variety.com/2022/film/news/prince-william-bafta-awards-2022-1235201205/ |url-status=live}}</ref>

The acceptance speeches during the ceremony were noted for their excessive length; Heritage found some of them overlong but several of them, instead, moving. He also noted that the speeches could afford to run long because the lack of winners present at the ceremony naturally cut down on speech time; Heritage was more critical towards the nominees for not attending, saying that "they [BAFTA] could have held this ceremony in a minivan for all the people who turned up to collect their awards" and, before the ''In Memoriam'' montage, that people featured in it "actually have a good excuse" for not being there.<ref name="Heritage"/> While hosting, Wilson made a poorly-received joke about the personal life of [[BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Leading Role|Best Actor in a Leading Role]] winner [[Will Smith]] and, at the response, commented that it was deserved as Smith had not shown up to the ceremony.<ref name="Power"/><ref>{{cite web |last=Harrison |first=Ellie |date=13 March 2022 |title='Come on, he never showed up!': Rebel Wilson jokes about Will Smith's marriage to Jada Pinkett Smith during Baftas |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/arts-entertainment/films/news/baftas-2022-will-smith-rebel-wilson-b2034953.html |access-date=14 March 2022 |work=[[The Independent]] |archive-date=13 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220313223707/https://www.independent.co.uk/arts-entertainment/films/news/baftas-2022-will-smith-rebel-wilson-b2034953.html |url-status=live}}</ref> The high number of absent nominees may be attributed to the ceremony taking place on the same day as the [[27th Critics' Choice Awards]], which rescheduled their ceremony shortly beforehand and stated that there were no other possible dates, acknowledging the clash and setting up a parallel venue in London (in addition to usual city Los Angeles) to encourage people to attend both ceremonies.<ref name="NME"/><ref>{{cite web |last=Lewis |first=Hilary |date=16 February 2022 |title=Critics Choice Awards to Take Place from London and Los Angeles |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/critics-choice-awards-2022-london-la-1235094799/ |access-date=13 March 2022 |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |archive-date=13 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220313231445/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/critics-choice-awards-2022-london-la-1235094799/ |url-status=live}}</ref>

In a series of long speeches, [[Lady Gaga]] introduced last year's [[BAFTA Rising Star Award|Rising Star Award]] winner, [[Bukky Bakray]], so that Bakray could present the award; [[Lashana Lynch]] ultimately won.<ref name="Dex"/><ref>{{cite press release |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/lady-gaga-to-introduce-ee-rising-star-moment-2022-ee-bafta-film-awards |title=Lady Gaga to introduce this year's EE Rising Star moment during next weekend's EE British Academy Film Awards Ceremony in London |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]] |date=4 March 2022 |access-date=15 March 2022 |archive-date=11 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220311060644/https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/lady-gaga-to-introduce-ee-rising-star-moment-2022-ee-bafta-film-awards |url-status=live}}</ref> Heritage was also critical of this, explaining his outlook by noting that Lynch had "already risen quite high", playing main characters in major movies, including ''[[Captain Marvel (film)|Captain Marvel]]'' (2019) (plus other [[Marvel Cinematic Universe]] entries) and the Bond film ''[[No Time to Die]]'' (2021), and so her career did not need the profile boost of the Rising Star Award.<ref name="Heritage"/> ''[[NME]]'' was more positive, writing that the award "underlines her rise".<ref name="NME"/> Lynch's acceptance speech referred to her family migrating as part of the [[British African-Caribbean people#The "Windrush generation"|Windrush generation]] and how they taught her "about failure, about noes and what noes mean and how to celebrate your yeses", preparing her for the [[film industry]].<ref name="Dex"/>

Unusually, none of the Best Actress nominees were also nominated in the [[Academy Award for Best Actress|same category]] at the [[94th Academy Awards]].<ref>{{cite web |last=Mountford |first=Fiona |title=Why BAFTA has shunned the Oscars A-list |url=https://www.spectator.co.uk/article/why-the-baftas-are-right-to-shun-the-a-list |access-date=13 March 2022 |work=[[The Spectator]] |date=9 February 2022 |url-access=limited |archive-date=13 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220313234449/https://www.spectator.co.uk/article/why-the-baftas-are-right-to-shun-the-a-list |url-status=live}}</ref> Additionally, of the actresses nominated for the BAFTA, only Lady Gaga had been nominated at the [[28th Screen Actors Guild Awards]].<ref name="Thompson">{{cite web |last=Thompson |first=Anne |author-link=Anne Thompson (film journalist) |date=3 February 2022 |title=What the BAFTA Nominations Mean for the Oscars: Watch Out for Lady Gaga |url=https://www.indiewire.com/awards/industry/bafta-nominations-oscars-1234696192/ |access-date=13 March 2022 |publisher=[[IndieWire]] |archive-date=8 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220308000206/https://www.indiewire.com/2022/02/bafta-nominations-oscars-1234696192/ |url-status=live}}</ref> American film journalist [[Anne Thompson (film journalist)|Anne Thompson]] described [[Joanna Scanlan]]'s nomination in the category as "bizarre"; Scanlan ultimately won and was surprised herself.<ref name="Dex"/><ref name="Thompson"/> In her speech, she thanked BAFTA in [[Welsh language|Welsh]].<ref name="Colderick"/> ''[[NME]]'' and British film critic [[Peter Bradshaw]] wrote that she was a deserving winner.<ref name="NME"/><ref name="Bradshaw">{{cite web |last=Bradshaw |first=Peter |author-link=Peter Bradshaw |date=13 March 2022 |title=Jane Campion leads roll-call of worthy winners as Baftas hit all the right notes |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2022/mar/13/jane-campion-winners-2022-baftas-hit-all-the-right-notes |access-date=14 March 2022 |work=[[The Guardian]] |url-access=limited |archive-date=14 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220314011939/https://www.theguardian.com/film/2022/mar/13/jane-campion-winners-2022-baftas-hit-all-the-right-notes |url-status=live}}</ref> In winning the award for [[BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Supporting Role|Best Actor in a Supporting Role]], ''CODA'' actor [[Troy Kotsur]] became the first [[deafness|deaf]] actor to win a BAFA, as well as the first deaf actor to win a BAFTA in a major category and first deaf male actor to win a BAFTA.<ref name="BBC News"/><!--If someone chooses to change instances of "BAFA" to "BAFTA" in this article for some reason, this instance would need extra clarifying as Kotsur is not the first deaf actor to win a BAFTA, only a BAFA.--><ref>{{cite web |date=14 March 2022 |title=Baftas 2022 ceremony – in pictures |work=[[The Guardian]] |url=https://www.theguardian.com/artanddesign/gallery/2022/mar/14/baftas-2022-ceremony-in-pictures |access-date=14 March 2022 |issn=0261-3077 |url-access=limited |archive-date=14 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220314082415/https://www.theguardian.com/artanddesign/gallery/2022/mar/14/baftas-2022-ceremony-in-pictures |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last=Gordon |first=Naomi |title=CODA star Troy Kotsur makes history with BAFTA 2022 win |url=https://www.radiotimes.com/movies/coda-troy-kotsur-bafta-2022-newsupdate/ |date=13 March 2022 |access-date=14 March 2022 |work=[[Radio Times]] |archive-date=13 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220313210952/https://www.radiotimes.com/movies/coda-troy-kotsur-bafta-2022-newsupdate/ |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite AV media |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vEPaHotRQXs |title=Troy Kotsur makes history as first deaf male actor to win a BAFTA {{!}} BAFTA Film Awards 2022 - BBC |publisher=[[BBC]] |via=[[YouTube]] |date=13 March 2022 |access-date=14 March 2022}}</ref> Bradshaw had worried that ''[[Belfast (film)|Belfast]]'' winning [[BAFTA Award for Outstanding British Film|Outstanding British Film]], and thus being labelled "British", could have been controversial, but was "very pleased" to see it was not significantly so, attributing this to the quality of the film.<ref name="Bradshaw"/>

Overall, Bradshaw felt that the winners were "well-judged and satisfying".<ref name="Bradshaw"/> Heritage also noted that the BAFA winners at the ceremony gave little fresh insight to the likely [[Academy Awards|Oscar]] winners, with a mix of frontrunner and left-field wins.<ref name="Heritage"/>

==In Memoriam==
The ''In Memoriam'' montage this year was played to the song "[[Send In the Clowns]]" by [[Stephen Sondheim]], who was included within the montage.<ref>{{cite web |last=Sheehan |first=Paul |date=13 March 2022 |title=BAFTAs: 'The Power of the Dog' wins Best Picture and Director, 'Dune' takes 5 |url=https://www.goldderby.com/article/2022/2022-bafta-awards-winners-dune-live-blog/ |access-date=14 March 2022 |publisher=Gold Derby |archive-date=13 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220313203853/https://www.goldderby.com/article/2022/2022-bafta-awards-winners-dune-live-blog/ |url-status=live}}</ref>
